mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs in complexity and resource requirements depending on the deployment scale.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

```

> This adds the vLLM repository for the latest release.

steps.title: Install the vLLM Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the required CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample vLLM resource to test the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This creates an example vLLM resource with 2 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> You should see the status of the vLLM resource as 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This will remove vLLM and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your vLLM pod.
> This upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the vLLM Helm chart is installed and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM CRDs may have been previously installed.

**Fix:**
Delete the existing CRDs before reinstalling: kubectl delete crd vllms.vllm.io

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster resources or adjust the resource requests in your vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
